mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) in regression against FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in private demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint negative signal fails to predict negative returns when RSAFS and HOUST both decline, with out-of-sample accuracy below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds its 2017 baseline during a tightening cycle, it confirms resilient manufacturing output that supports equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns do not materialize when INDPRO exceeds 100 during tightening periods, with a mean return difference indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index above 300 reflects entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or flat when CPIAUCSL exceeds 300, contradicting the inflation-risk premium mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls consistently correlate with expanding economic activity and higher corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS changes falls below 0.02, or quarterly forward returns show no positive correlation with payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Negative real GDP growth combined with falling real personal consumption expenditures confirms a contractionary demand shock that depresses earnings forecasts and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns when both series decline, with a hit rate below 40% over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production increase exceeding 0.5 percent signals rapid supply-side recovery that boosts market confidence and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive when industrial production growth exceeds the 0.5 percent threshold, with t-statistic below 1.5.
